task_completion.description: Load the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset into Oracle Analytics Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Oracle Analytics Desktop UI is visible with the 'sample_order_lines2023' dataset or its columns (e.g., Product Category, Customer Segment, Sales) displayed in the Data panel on the left.
A Pivot Table visualization structure (a grid layout designed for cross-tabulation) is present on the main canvas, and the visualization type icon in the Grammar panel indicates a Pivot Table.

task_completion.description: Configure the Pivot Table with Product Category in rows and Customer Segment in columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Grammar panel, 'Product Category' is placed in the 'Rows' section and 'Customer Segment' is placed in the 'Columns' section. The pivot table grid reflects this layout.

task_completion.description: Configure the Pivot Table to display Revenue (Sales) values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Sales' measure is placed in the 'Values' section of the Grammar panel, and the pivot table grid is populated with numerical data.

task_completion.description: Title the visualization 'Revenue by Category and Segment'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title text displayed directly above the Pivot Table visualization on the canvas reads exactly 'Revenue by Category and Segment'.

task_completion.description: Save the workbook as 'Logistics_Revenue_Pivot' in the Documents folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top application bar displays the project name as 'Logistics_Revenue_Pivot', or a save dialog/file explorer shows the file 'Logistics_Revenue_Pivot.dva' being saved in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to create the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface being actively used (menus, drag-and-drop, panels), rather than terminal commands or scripts generating the file.

integrity.description: The visualization was created in the correct software and not mocked up.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface is clearly Oracle Analytics Desktop, not Microsoft Excel, a web browser, or an image editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply rename an existing file or use terminal commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the step-by-step construction of the pivot table and a legitimate 'Save' action within the application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains a 'Sales' column instead of a 'Revenue' column; the agent must use 'Sales' for the values. The expected saved filename is 'Logistics_Revenue_Pivot.dva'. The visualization title must be exactly 'Revenue by Category and Segment'. The visualization type must be a Pivot Table.
